rulings.Case Title: WALTER MORRIS VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, September 13, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Defendant Decker Auto Supply, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant did not satisfy its burden of production that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that he was exposed to an asbestos-containing product sold by defendant or that any such exposure was a substantial factor in causing or contributing to his asbestos-related illness. Defendant¿s failure to demonstrate that plaintiff responded to written contention discovery precludes defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: JAMES NASH VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, September 13, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 2. Defendant Merlex Stucco, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is granted. No opposition filed. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: WILLIAM M. MCRAY et al VS. 3M COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Strike Plaintiffs' Complaint For Personal Injury
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, September 13, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 3. Defendant John Crane, Inc.'s motion to strike portions of plaintiffs' complaint is off calendar as moot given the ruling on defendant's demurrer. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: WILLIAM M. MCRAY et al VS. 3M COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Strike Loss Of Consortium From Plaintiffs Complaint For Personal Injury And Loss Of Consortium - Asbestos
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, September 13, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 3. Defendant Crane Co.¿s motion to strike loss of consortium from plaintiffs¿ complaint is off calendar as moot given the ruling on defendant¿s demurrer. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: WILLIAM M. MCRAY et al VS. 3M COMPANY et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, September 13, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 3. Defendant John Crane, Inc.¿s general demurrer to plaintiffs¿ complaint is sustained with leave to amend and its special demurrer to the sixth cause of action for loss of consortium is sustained without leave to amend. The general demurrer is sustained with ten days leave to amend to give plaintiffs an opportunity, if they are able to do so, to allege facts sufficient to meet the requirements of Bockrath and of C.C.P. § 340.2 (statute of limitations). The special demurrer to the sixth cause of action is sustained without leave to amend. Since all of Mr. McRay¿s claimed exposure to asbestos pre-dated his marriage with Mrs. McRay, Mrs. McRay does not have a viable claim for loss of consortium. Plaintiffs¿ argument that Zwicker is inapplicable is belied by the language of Zwicker. (See also Owens-Illinois, Inc. v. Cook (MD 2005) 872 A.2d 969, 982-84 (an asbestos exposure case which refers to Zwicker as among those cases that hold that ¿the marriage requirement for a loss of consortium claim is absolute, [and] that if the marriage post-dated the injury, there is no consortium claim.¿)). If plaintiffs choose to file an amended complaint, the court encourages plaintiffs to address defendant¿s arguments regarding the application of CCP 340.2, but the court is not now making any ruling on that issue. = (220/HEK)
